context: This chapter describes the sapindikarana ritual performed on the twelfth or thirteenth day after death, in which the departed soul is ceremonially merged with the ancestors. It explains the merging of pindas, the significance of this final ceremony, and the prayers offered for the eternal peace of the soul.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
सपिण्डीकरणं ब्रूहि यदा प्रेतः पितॄन् व्रजेत् ।
तत्कर्मविधिमाख्याहि भगवन् परमेश्वर ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuda uvācha —
sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ brūhi yadā pretaḥ pitṝn vrajet |
tat-karma-vidhim ākhyāhi bhagavan parameśvara ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: Describe the sapindikarana ceremony, by which the departed joins the ancestors. Tell me the procedure of that rite, O Blessed Supreme Lord.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
शृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि सपिण्डीकरणं शुभम् ।
येन प्रेतत्वमुत्सृज्य पितृत्वं प्राप्नुयात् ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvācha —
śṛṇu tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ śubham |
yena pretatvam utsṛjya pitṛtvaṃ prāpnuyāt dhruvam ||
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarkhsya, I shall describe the auspicious sapindikarana ceremony, by which the departed abandons the state of a restless spirit and certainly attains the status of an ancestor.

verses.hindi:
द्वादशे दिवसे कार्यं सपिण्डीकरणं बुधैः ।
त्रयोदशे वा कर्तव्यं विधिवत् तु विचक्षणैः ॥
verses.transliteration:
dvādaśe divase kāryaṃ sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ budhaiḥ |
trayodaśe vā kartavyaṃ vidhivat tu vicakṣaṇaiḥ ||
verses.translation: The sapindikarana should be performed by the wise on the twelfth day, or by the discerning on the thirteenth day, according to the prescribed rites.

verses.hindi:
प्रेतपिण्डं पृथक् कृत्वा पितृपिण्डत्रयं तथा ।
चत्वारः पिण्डकाः कार्याः सतिलाः कुशसंयुताः ॥
verses.transliteration:
preta-piṇḍaṃ pṛthak kṛtvā pitṛ-piṇḍa-trayaṃ tathā |
catvāraḥ piṇḍakāḥ kāryāḥ sa-tilāḥ kuśa-saṃyutāḥ ||
verses.translation: The pinda of the departed should be made separately, and three pindas for the ancestors likewise. Thus four pindas should be prepared, mixed with sesame and placed on kusha grass.

verses.hindi:
प्रथमः पिण्ड प्रेतस्य द्वितीयः पितुरेव च ।
तृतीयः पितामहस्य चतुर्थः प्रपितामहात् ॥
verses.transliteration:
prathamaḥ piṇḍa pretasya dvitīyaḥ pitureva ca |
tṛtīyaḥ pitāmahasya caturthaḥ prapitāmahāt ||
verses.translation: The first pinda is for the departed, the second for the father, the third for the grandfather, and the fourth for the great-grandfather.

verses.hindi:
प्रेतपिण्डं ततो भित्वा त्रिधा कुर्याद् विचक्षणः ।
पितृपिण्डत्रये चैव तं भागं संनियोजयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
preta-piṇḍaṃ tato bhitvā tridhā kuryād vicakṣaṇaḥ |
pitṛ-piṇḍa-traye caiva taṃ bhāgaṃ sanniyojayet ||
verses.translation: The discerning one should then divide the pinda of the departed into three parts and merge each part with the three pindas of the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
प्रेतपिण्डस्य संयोगात् पितृपिण्डे विधानतः ।
एकीभावं व्रजत्यात्मा पितृलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
preta-piṇḍasya saṃyogāt pitṛ-piṇḍe vidhānataḥ |
ekī-bhāvaṃ vrajatyātmā pitṛ-lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: By the union of the pinda of the departed with the ancestral pindas according to the prescribed rites, the soul becomes one with them and goes to the world of the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
जलपात्राणि चत्वारि तथा कार्याणि सूरिभिः ।
प्रेतपात्रजलं भित्वा मिश्रयेत् पितृपात्रके ॥
verses.transliteration:
jala-pātrāṇi catvāri tathā kāryāṇi sūribhiḥ |
preta-pātra-jalaṃ bhitvā miśrayet pitṛ-pātrake ||
verses.translation: Four vessels of water should likewise be prepared by the learned. The water from the vessel of the departed should be divided and mixed into the vessels of the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
सपिण्डीकरणात् पूर्वं प्रेत इत्युच्यते बुधैः ।
सपिण्डीकरणात् पश्चात् पितृत्वं प्राप्नुयाद् ध्रुवम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
sapiṇḍīkaraṇāt pūrvaṃ preta ityuchyate budhaiḥ |
sapiṇḍīkaraṇāt paścāt pitṛtvaṃ prāpnuyād dhruvam ||
verses.translation: Before the sapindikarana, the departed is called 'preta' (restless spirit) by the wise. After the sapindikarana, the soul certainly attains the status of an ancestor (pitri).

verses.hindi:
एष एव विधिस्तार्क्ष्य प्रेतत्वविनिवर्तकः ।
यावत् सपिण्डीकरणं तावत् प्रेतत्वमश्नुते ॥
verses.transliteration:
eṣa eva vidhistārkṣya pretatva-vinivartakaḥ |
yāvat sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ tāvat pretatvam aśnute ||
verses.translation: This indeed is the rite, O Tarkhsya, that removes the state of being a restless spirit. Until the sapindikarana is performed, the soul experiences the condition of a preta.

verses.hindi:
ततो ब्राह्मणभोज्यं तु षड्रसोपेतमुत्तमम् ।
पायसान्नं घृतोपेतं दद्यात् विप्रसभासु च ॥
verses.transliteration:
tato brāhmaṇa-bhojyaṃ tu ṣaḍ-rasopetam uttamam |
pāyasānnaṃ ghṛtopetaṃ dadyāt vipra-sabhāsu ca ||
verses.translation: Then the best food with six flavors should be offered to the brahmins. Rice pudding prepared with ghee should be given in the assemblies of the learned.

verses.hindi:
वस्त्रं सुवर्णं रजतं गाश्च दद्यात् सदक्षिणम् ।
प्रेतमुद्दिश्य विप्रेभ्यो दत्तं स्वर्गप्रदायकम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
vastraṃ suvarṇaṃ rajataṃ gāśca dadyāt sa-dakṣiṇam |
pretam uddiśya viprebhyo dattaṃ svarga-pradāyakam ||
verses.translation: Garments, gold, silver, and cows should be given along with a monetary fee. Given on behalf of the departed to the brahmins, it bestows heaven.

verses.hindi:
नारायणबलिं दद्यात् सपिण्डीकरणे सदा ।
नारायणात्मकः प्रेतो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
nārāyaṇa-baliṃ dadyāt sapiṇḍīkaraṇe sadā |
nārāyaṇātmakaḥ preto viṣṇu-lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: The Narayana bali offering should always be made at the sapindikarana ceremony. The departed, becoming one with Narayana in essence, goes to the realm of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
एकोद्दिष्टं ततः श्राद्धं कुर्यात् प्रेतस्य मुक्तये ।
पार्वणं च ततः श्राद्धं पितॄणां तर्पणं भवेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
ekoddiṣṭaṃ tataḥ śrāddhaṃ kuryāt pretasya muktaye |
pārvaṇaṃ ca tataḥ śrāddhaṃ pitṝṇāṃ tarpaṇaṃ bhavet ||
verses.translation: The ekoddishta shraddha should be performed for the liberation of the departed. Then the parvana shraddha should be done, which serves as the libation for the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
सपिण्डीकृतमात्रस्तु प्रेतो विगतकल्मषः ।
पितृलोकगतो भूत्वा श्राद्धान्नेन प्रतर्प्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
sapiṇḍīkṛta-mātrastu preto vigata-kalmaṣaḥ |
pitṛ-loka-gato bhūtvā śrāddhānnena pratarpyate ||
verses.translation: The moment the sapindikarana is performed, the spirit becomes free from all taint. Having reached the world of the ancestors, it is nourished by the food offered at shraddha ceremonies.

verses.hindi:
ततस्तिलैः कुशैश्चैव गन्धपुष्पैरलङ्कृतम् ।
विप्रान् सम्पूज्य विधिवत् पिण्डान् दद्यात् समाहितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
tatastilaiḥ kuśaiścaiva gandha-puṣpair alaṅkṛtam |
viprān sampūjya vidhivat piṇḍān dadyāt samāhitaḥ ||
verses.translation: Then, adorned with sesame, kusha grass, and fragrant flowers, having worshiped the brahmins according to the rites, one should offer the pindas with concentration.

verses.hindi:
प्रेतस्योद्देशतः कृत्वा सपिण्डीकरणं शुभम् ।
ततः श्राद्धानि कुर्वीत वार्षिकाणि यथाविधि ॥
verses.transliteration:
pretasyoddeśataḥ kṛtvā sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ śubham |
tataḥ śrāddhāni kurvīta vārṣikāṇi yathāvidhi ||
verses.translation: Having performed the auspicious sapindikarana on behalf of the departed, one should then conduct the annual shraddha ceremonies according to the prescribed method.

verses.hindi:
सपिण्डीकरणं कर्म सर्वपापप्रणाशनम् ।
प्रेतमुक्तिकरं चैव पितृसन्तोषकारणम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ karma sarva-pāpa-praṇāśanam |
preta-mukti-karaṃ caiva pitṛ-santoṣa-kāraṇam ||
verses.translation: The sapindikarana ceremony destroys all sins, grants liberation to the departed, and is the cause of satisfaction to the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
सपिण्डीकरणं यस्तु न करोति विमोहितः ।
तस्य प्रेतो भवेन्नित्यं कुलं चास्य विनश्यति ॥
verses.transliteration:
sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ yastu na karoti vimohitaḥ |
tasya preto bhavennityaṃ kulaṃ cāsya vinaśyati ||
verses.translation: He who, being deluded, does not perform the sapindikarana — his departed one remains a restless spirit forever, and his family line is destroyed.

verses.hindi:
ततो विसर्जनं कुर्यात् ब्राह्मणानां विधानतः ।
आशीर्वादं गृहीत्वा तु गृहं गच्छेत् समाहितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
tato visarjanaṃ kuryāt brāhmaṇānāṃ vidhānataḥ |
āśīrvādaṃ gṛhītvā tu gṛhaṃ gacchet samāhitaḥ ||
verses.translation: Then the brahmins should be ceremonially dismissed according to the rules. Having received their blessings, one should return home with a focused mind.

verses.hindi:
गृहशुद्धिं ततः कुर्यात् गोमयेन च लेपनम् ।
पञ्चगव्यप्रशनं च शान्तिहोमं च कारयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
gṛha-śuddhiṃ tataḥ kuryāt gomayena ca lepanam |
pañca-gavya-praśanaṃ ca śānti-homaṃ ca kārayet ||
verses.translation: Then the house should be purified with cow dung plaster. The partaking of panchagavya and a peace fire ritual should also be performed.

verses.hindi:
एवं कृते विधानेन सपिण्डीकरणे शुभे ।
प्रेतो विमुक्तिमाप्नोति पितृलोके महीयते ॥
verses.transliteration:
evaṃ kṛte vidhānena sapiṇḍīkaraṇe śubhe |
preto vimuktim āpnoti pitṛ-loke mahīyate ||
verses.translation: When the auspicious sapindikarana is thus performed according to the proper rites, the departed attains liberation and is honored in the world of the ancestors.

verses.hindi:
इत्येतत् कथितं तार्क्ष्य सपिण्डीकरणं विधिम् ।
सर्वपापहरं पुण्यं प्रेतमोक्षप्रदायकम् ।
यः शृणोति पठेद् वापि स मुक्तो नात्र संशयः ॥
verses.transliteration:
ityetat kathitaṃ tārkṣya sapiṇḍīkaraṇaṃ vidhim |
sarva-pāpa-haraṃ puṇyaṃ preta-mokṣa-pradāyakam |
yaḥ śṛṇoti paṭhed vāpi sa mukto nātra saṃśayaḥ ||
verses.translation: Thus, O Tarkhsya, I have described the rite of sapindikarana, which removes all sins, is meritorious, and grants liberation to the departed. He who hears or recites this is freed — there is no doubt.
